summary: Follow-up cycle addressing external review #1 (berendt) on CC-0091. The implementer (1) gated FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets emission on ESO adoption state by extending hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets in keystone_controller.go:419-439 to also skip PushSecrets lacking the ESO cleanup finalizer, restoring the CC-0079 exactly-once contract across the new Pass-0 adoption-wait window; (2) added a stage-1 integration-test assertion in integration_test.go:3304-3323 that lists namespace Events, filters by InvolvedObject.UID and Reason==FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets, and requires count<=1; (3) rewrote the misleading doc comment on addESOAdoptionFinalizerToPushSecret (integration_test.go:2940-2950) to accurately describe that any finalizer blocks etcd removal; (4) added Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) in SetupWithManager (keystone_controller.go:599) so the adoption-wait latency collapses from 15s polling to watch-event delivery; and (5) tightened TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_MixedAdoptionState from BeNumerically('>=', 2) to Equal(2) at reconcile_secrets_test.go:1294. go vet clean, go build clean, all unit tests green (including -race). Integration test skipped due to sandbox lacking envtest binaries.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests pass with -race (go test -race ./internal/controller/ → ok 8.284s)
Integration test compiles with -tags=integration (go vet -tags=integration clean)
Integration test TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption runs in envtest (skipped in sandbox — envtest binaries unavailable; requires manual verification)
Stage-1 event-count assertion added with UID+Reason filter and <=1 bound
MixedAdoptionState assertion tightened to Equal(2) to catch any extra Delete calls
Existing TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_* and CC-0079 happy path still pass (full controller test suite ok)

code_quality_checklist.item: Doc comments on reconcileDeleteOpenBao and hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets accurately describe the three-disqualifier logic and cite CC-0091/REQ-007
hasESOFinalizer helper is reused (already defined in reconcile_secrets.go:295) — no duplication introduced by the controller-side check at keystone_controller.go:433
Owns(PushSecret) comment explains WHY (latency collapse) with feature ID
Helper doc-comment rewrite on addESOAdoptionFinalizerToPushSecret is technically accurate

security_checklist.item: No RBAC expansion — neither keystone_controller.go nor the tests introduce new kubebuilder:rbac markers; get/delete on pushsecrets remain the only extra verbs
No secret material in logs or error messages

Adoption gate preserves the primary security property (no Delete issued before ESO adoption) — verified by TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_MixedAdoptionState asserting deleteCount==0 in the mixed-state case

architecture_checklist.item: Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) is the correct fix for I-001 — EnsurePushSecret in internal/common/secrets/secrets.go sets a controller reference so ownerReferences[].kind=Keystone, unlike ESO-managed ExternalSecret children (which would fail the watch-handler-filter-must-match-actual-ownership-model review pattern)

Single source of truth for ESO finalizer string (esoPushSecretFinalizer in reconcile_secrets.go) is preserved — keystone_controller.go:433 reuses the hasESOFinalizer helper rather than duplicating the literal
Three-pass handler contract in reconcile_secrets.go unchanged; only hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets in keystone_controller.go is extended

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated ESO-finalizer-detection logic — keystone_controller.go:433 calls hasESOFinalizer from reconcile_secrets.go:295
No speculative caching of Pass-0 Get in Pass-2 (I-002 correctly deferred per reviewer guidance — 2-element loop makes it premature optimization)

fail_fast_checklist.item: hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets returns early on the first live+adopted PushSecret (short-circuit in for-loop at line 436)
Integration-test assertion fails loudly with a descriptive message citing CC-0079/CC-0091

defensive_checklist.item: hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets handles NotFound, Terminating, and Unadopted PushSecrets explicitly — no implicit fall-through
Event assertion filters by both InvolvedObject.UID and Reason — avoids counting unrelated events from other CRs in the same namespace

issues_found.description: Misleading comment in integration_test.go:3308 references a variable name (`deleteCount`) that does not exist in this scope. The local counter is `stage1Finalizing` (declared at line 3315 and asserted at line 3321). Likely a copy-paste artifact from a Delete-count test (e.g. TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_MixedAdoptionState which does use `deleteCount`). Reader confusion risk — the reader looks for `deleteCount` and doesn't find it, weakening the comment's usefulness as a regression-narrative.
issues_found.fix: Replace `would surface as deleteCount>1 here` with `would surface as stage1Finalizing>1 here`.

suggested_improvements: Consider a follow-up assertion in TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption that also bounds FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets emissions across the full termination (stages 1+2+3) at a small constant (e.g., <=2), which would also protect against the staggered-adoption edge case where each partial adoption triggers a fresh Pass-1 attempt.
next_steps: Address the minor comment inaccuracy at integration_test.go:3308 (stage1Finalizing, not deleteCount) — AUTO_FIX, one-word change.

Run the integration test TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption in a real envtest environment (outside the sandbox) to confirm the stage-1 event-count assertion behaves as expected — the sandbox skip is acknowledged but not verified.
Per the original acceptance criteria, run the chainsaw/keystone-deletion-cleanup workflow 10 consecutive times to confirm flake resolution.
